_doc: WAC 314-55-155 banned-claim corpus. Loaded by scripts/check-wac-314-55-155-banned-claims.mjs. Edit to expand corpus or add allowlist exceptions. NO code changes needed when this JSON evolves.

corpus._doc: Each entry: pattern (regex source, case-insensitive), rule (description). The gate combines them into a single sweep.

patterns.rule: FDA-approved claim (only OK in defensive disclaimer)

allowlistFiles.reason: Audit-batch-1 deferred fix: bestFor arrays use 'Pain relief' / 'Stress relief' bare phrases. Doug/Kat review queue in WAC_314_55_155_AUDIT_2026_05_26.md. Remove this allowlist after rewrite-batch-2 ships.
Changelog comments quote banned terms when describing prior compliance fixes.
Defense-layer file — by definition lists banned terms.
Server-side scanner that LOOKS FOR violations.
Defensive matcher for customer-said phrases (e.g., 'i have cancer' redirect-to-staff).
Defensive disclaimer copy ('Cannabis isn't FDA-approved', 'Cannabis is not a substitute for diagnosis') + statutory RCW 69.51A.010 MMJ-program qualifying-condition recitation.
Blog corpus — defensive disclaimer footers quote 'FDA-approved' in NEGATING context.
Strain-type page doctrine file — comments list banned phrases for compliance reference. Pinned by lib/__tests__/strain-types.test.ts at v39.245.

Defensive header comment — explicitly avoids therapeutic claims.
Defensive header comment — explicitly avoids 'similar effects' / 'treats same condition' framing.

Code comment about 'prevents' (non-medical) + 'Dutch Treat' strain name.
